findings.description: Single rule conflates two distinct directives with opposite polarity: (a) a recommended/allowed list of known-safe color pairings (must/should-style guidance) and (b) an explicit ban on gold/sunshine text on white (must_not). Merging them under one hardness/polarity (hard_constraint/must_not) misrepresents the permissive first half as a prohibition and loses the nuance that golds are still permitted for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis.

new_rules.text: Use known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.
Do not use Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast); golds are reserved for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

findings.description: Source unit 'Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning' is not represented by any candidate rule — a full directive is missing from the cluster, leaving accessibility guidance uncovered.

payload.text: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

findings.description: Rule captures the WCAG AA numeric thresholds but omits the governance facet naming the verification method/tool (webaim.com or equivalent) and the required scope ('every text/background pair'), which are needed to make the constraint auditable/enforceable.
